best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest contributor: 31 trades, $2415.3 PnL, 252.47 PnL/exposure hour, no slow >=12h trades, and worst MAE -403.2. Prior pullback throughput re-expansion hurt badly, so protect current boundaries.
breakout_long_1dte adds 20 trades and $1305 PnL when 0DTE is unavailable. Prior direct 1DTE tightening reduced score and PnL, so preserve the edge while searching for a separate 1DTE companion/split.

charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 5 trades but contributes $985.6, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, and no slow trades. Prior structure tightening reduced score without improving worst MAE.

squeeze_long contributes $721 over 10 trades with worst MAE -208.25 and no slow trades. Recent TP expansion check was flat from the current leader, so do not force further upside changes on risk-reducer track.
call_wall_acceptance_long has one trade, $91 PnL, -11.7 MAE, and 455 PnL/exposure hour. The evidence is too small for promotion but clean enough for isolated sample-building, not broad relaxation.

broken_edges.evidence: All top runs share the same leader metrics, while hidden signature candidates retain only 72-84% PnL and often leave worst MAE unchanged. Single-threshold setup floor >=62 would block 56 trades and retain only 45% PnL.
broken_edges.problem: Bad-MAE rows overlap too much with profitable winners; broad filters are not acceptable for a risk-adjusted leader.

broken_edges.evidence: 1DTE local quality gates lost $317-$558 and worsened worst MAE to -603.75 in prior experiments despite removing trades.
broken_edges.problem: The profitable 1DTE fallback is fragile under direct structure/slope tightening.

broken_edges.evidence: The prior pullback re-expansion added little net profit but worsened drawdown to 2.0%, worst MAE to -2026.5, and introduced a slow >=12h trade.
broken_edges.problem: Wider pullback throughput can create extreme no-stop-loss heat.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE and EXPIRED_0DTE samples are tiny but positive; no-0DTE profiles are not the source of current -527 MAE.
broken_edges.problem: Fallback edits risk overfitting small samples and do not address the main tail.

broken_edges.action: freeze dormant disabled standard active profiles
broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long produced zero trades in this leader context.
broken_edges.problem: No active evidence to optimize; enabling them would be discovery, not risk reduction.

market_regime_read: The current leader is a strong long-only, positive-exit regime: 107 trades, $7125.8 net PnL, 100% realized win rate, 0.4% drawdown, no slow >=12h trades, and 36.17 exposure hours. The risk problem is not realized losses but hidden no-stop-loss heat: average MAE about -101.7 and worst MAE -527. The largest tail is charm_pin_pullback_long, but bad-MAE fingerprints overlap heavily with protected fast and extended-VWAP winners, so the next risk-reducer plan should favor surgical veto-boundary validation and profile splits over broad entry pruning.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separates profitable charm-pin extension recoveries from delayed no-progress heat.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: A crude classifier may overfit one -527 row or block fast extended-VWAP winners that have median MAE near -14.9.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin High-Extension Recovery Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Entry-state features plus early-path features: ES-VWAP distance, VWAP acceleration, pullback-from-high, rolling-high distance, RSI/ADX slope, first 1-5 minutes MFE/MAE, and TP-touch latency.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long with high ES-VWAP extension, weak/negative acceleration, large pullback or rolling-high distance, and delayed early profit.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE from -527 toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL and >=95% protected fast winner count.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Improves 1DTE risk by routing pullback-like 1DTE states separately instead of tightening breakout_long_1dte.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May be unreachable or may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades if router precedence overlaps.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades with ES reclaim/pullback state, option map availability, forward DEX, structure score, VWAP slope, and 1DTE selected TP behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1305, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.86, and total PnL remains >=98% of leader.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Config-only dormant concept to test whether pin-avoid logic can absorb tail-heavy charm/pin states without broad pruning.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant profile may steal profitable charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long entries while adding too few independent trades.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Late-Day/Charm Pin Avoid Holdout
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router activation holdout for late_day_pin_avoid_long or charm_wall_pin_avoid_long with profile PnL, overlap against charm_pin_pullback_long, MAE, and fast-winner cannibalization checks.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pin/charm context with extension/rejection and poor early follow-through risk.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile must be independently positive, reduce combined pin/charm worst MAE, and retain >=97% total PnL.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Finds whether clean call-wall acceptance can be scaled without weakening the main pullback/squeeze engines.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: One-trade evidence may be luck; relaxed gates may add noisy wall-chase entries or steal stronger signals.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Sample Builder
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance entries with DEX, forward DEX ratio, call-volume expansion, wall distance, MAE, and profile overlap.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Accepted call-wall context with positive net/forward DEX and call-volume expansion near upside strike.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 5 trades, positive profile PnL, average MAE better than -60, worst MAE better than -100, total PnL >=98% of leader.

next_experiments.config_bias: Nudge only the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection miss boundaries enough to capture the observed near misses, then reject if protected extended-VWAP winners or fast winners are materially blocked.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce worst MAE from -527 toward <= -403 without broad throughput loss.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The current extended-VWAP rejection veto nearly matched all bad-MAE rows; small boundary nudges may catch the tail while preserving the core edge.
next_experiments.name: Extended VWAP Near-Miss Boundary Validation
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, total PnL retention >=97%, completed trades down <=5, protected fast-winner PnL retention >=95%.
next_experiments.risk: The same signature covers clean extended-VWAP winners, so a loose boundary may reduce score more than it reduces risk.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use profile-scoped charm-pin tail-risk boundaries rather than global setup floors. Focus on high extension, large rolling-high/pullback distance, weak RSI/ADX context, and delayed-profit proxies where legal.
next_experiments.goal: Isolate the -527 charm-pin heat while preserving the 34-trade charm-pin contribution.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst tail has a distinct charm-pin high-extension/no-early-progress signature, but broad setup quality or structure filters are too destructive.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin Tail Box Micro-Refinement
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE, profile PnL retention >=90%, total PnL retention >=98%, fast_under_15m trades down <=3.
next_experiments.risk: The prior charm-pin tail work is already near a plateau; further tightening may be flat or block profitable recoveries.

next_experiments.config_bias: Avoid pullback TP/VWAP expansion. Test only narrow weak-structure plus delayed-profit risk proxies, and require strong PnL retention because pullback_long is the main engine.
next_experiments.goal: Improve pullback_long average MAE and reduce the -403 tail without sacrificing the largest PnL source.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Pullback_long tails show weak structure and slow-to-profit fingerprints, but broad score-raising removes too many good pullbacks.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Isolation Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: pullback_long worst MAE, average MAE, profile PnL retention >=92%, total score non-negative versus leader.
next_experiments.risk: Current config keys may not express early-path delay; broad proxies may block profitable weak-structure pullbacks.

next_experiments.config_bias: Activate pullback_long_1dte only as a controlled router/profile split, not as a direct replacement for breakout_long_1dte. Keep breakout_long_1dte protected and measure combined 1DTE results.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether VALID_1DTE_ONLY contains a lower-MAE pullback/reclaim subset.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Prior direct 1DTE tightening failed because 1DTE needs a companion profile rather than stricter breakout gates.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Activation
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1305, 1DTE average MAE improves, total PnL >=98% of leader.
next_experiments.risk: Router overlap may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte entries or produce no reachable trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Run as isolated sample-builder only. Do not relax broad router lists or pullback/squeeze gates in the same experiment.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether call_wall_acceptance_long is a real low-MAE edge or a one-trade artifact.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The single call-wall acceptance trade is exceptionally clean, but needs sample expansion before any promotion.
next_experiments.name: Call Wall Acceptance Clean Sample Builder
next_experiments.primary_metric: Profile trades >=5, positive PnL, worst MAE better than -100, total PnL >=98% of leader.
next_experiments.risk: Loosening acceptance may admit wall-chase noise and displace stronger pullback/squeeze trades.

research_notes: The leader has repeated identical top results, suggesting the current configuration is a stable local optimum. The remaining risk is hidden MAE, not realized loss or slow 12h exposure. All 24 bad-MAE rows are covered by the extended-VWAP near-miss family, but protected extended-VWAP winners are real, so the optimizer should avoid broad ES-VWAP max-distance, setup quality, structure score, or profile-removal experiments. The best next work is surgical: validate tiny veto-boundary nudges, isolate charm-pin and pullback tails by profile, and test a 1DTE companion rather than direct 1DTE tightening.
risk_controls: Reject any experiment with total PnL retention below 97-98% unless worst MAE improves dramatically and trade count remains healthy.
Reject any broad filter that blocks protected fast winners or extended-VWAP winners without improving worst MAE below -403.
Do not accept any experiment that introduces slow >=12h trades or worsens drawdown above the current 0.4% leader baseline.
Treat 100% win rate as non-protective; prioritize MAE, exposure hours, and time-to-profit diagnostics.
Keep fallback/no-0DTE edits isolated from main risk-reducer work because stale/expired samples are small and not the current tail source.
